breaking_news.title: Google patches two Chrome zero-days under active attack
breaking_news.summary:
Google has released emergency out-of-band updates to patch two high-severity zero-day vulnerabilities in the Chrome browser, tracked as CVE-2026-3909 and CVE-2026-3910. These flaws affect the Skia 2D graphics library and the V8 JavaScript engine, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or trigger memory corruption via crafted HTML pages.

CISA has added these flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, confirming that active exploitation is occurring in the wild against users. This widespread threat necessitates immediate patching for all Chrome users to prevent potential compromise of sensitive data and system control.

breaking_news.title: ShinyHunters claims 1 petabyte data theft from Telus Digital
breaking_news.summary:
Canadian outsourcing firm Telus Digital has admitted to a massive cyberattack by the extortion group ShinyHunters, resulting in the theft of up to one petabyte of data. The stolen information includes support recordings, source code, and employee records, marking one of the largest data exfiltrations reported in recent months.

ShinyHunters specializes in targeting Salesforce and SaaS vendors, and this breach highlights the significant risks faced by business process outsourcing providers handling sensitive client data. The sheer volume of stolen data poses long-term privacy and reputational risks for Telus and its corporate clients.

breaking_news.title: Veeam patches critical RCE flaws in Backup & Replication
breaking_news.summary:
Backup vendor Veeam has issued critical security updates to address multiple vulnerabilities in its widely used Backup & Replication platform, including three critical remote code execution (RCE) flaws. These vulnerabilities, rated 9.9 on the CVSS scale, allow authenticated users to execute code on backup servers, potentially undermining disaster recovery capabilities.

Compromising backup infrastructure is a primary objective for ransomware operators, making these flaws a high-priority target for attackers seeking to prevent data restoration. Organizations relying on Veeam for data protection must apply the patches immediately to secure their recovery environments against potential ransomware deployment.

breaking_news.title: Storm-2561 spoofs enterprise VPN clients to steal credentials
breaking_news.summary:
Microsoft has identified a sophisticated credential theft campaign by the threat actor Storm-2561, which uses SEO poisoning to distribute fake enterprise VPN clients. The attackers spoof legitimate download pages for vendors like Cisco, Fortinet, and Ivanti, redirecting users to malicious ZIP archives containing trojanized installers.

This campaign targets corporate employees searching for VPN software, stealing login credentials to gain unauthorized access to enterprise networks. The use of digitally signed malware and trusted vendor branding makes detection difficult, posing a significant risk to organizational security perimeters.

breaking_news.title: CrackArmor vulnerabilities expose 12.6M Linux systems to root access
breaking_news.summary:
Qualys researchers have disclosed nine critical vulnerabilities in the Linux kernel's AppArmor module, collectively named CrackArmor, which could allow unprivileged users to escalate to root. These confused deputy flaws enable attackers to bypass container isolation guarantees and circumvent kernel protections on affected systems.

The vulnerabilities impact approximately 12.6 million Linux systems globally, including cloud environments and containerized applications that rely on AppArmor for security. System administrators must prioritize patching to prevent potential privilege escalation attacks that could lead to full system compromise.

breaking_news.title: AppsFlyer Web SDK hijacked to spread crypto-stealing code
breaking_news.summary:
The AppsFlyer Web SDK was temporarily hijacked in a supply-chain attack that injected malicious JavaScript code designed to steal cryptocurrency from users. This incident demonstrates how compromised software development kits can be weaponized to distribute malware across a wide range of websites and applications.

The attack highlights the fragility of third-party dependencies in the web ecosystem, where a single compromised SDK can impact numerous downstream clients. Developers and security teams need to monitor SDK integrity closely to prevent similar supply-chain compromises in the future.

breaking_news.title: GlassWorm abuses 72 Open VSX extensions in supply-chain attack
breaking_news.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new iteration of the GlassWorm campaign that abuses 72 Open VSX extensions to target developers through a supply-chain attack. The threat actor is now utilizing extensionPack and extensionDependencies to turn standalone extensions into transitive delivery mechanisms for malware.

This escalation in propagation techniques allows the attackers to infect developer environments more effectively, potentially compromising code repositories and build pipelines. Organizations must audit their development tools and extension sources to mitigate the risk of this sophisticated supply-chain intrusion.

breaking_news.title: Authorities disrupt SocksEscort proxy service tied to AVrecon botnet
breaking_news.summary:
US and European authorities have disrupted the SocksEscort proxy service, which utilized the AVrecon botnet to hijack approximately 360,000 residential routers and IoT devices. The service allowed cybercriminals to route traffic through compromised systems to mask illegal activities, including phishing and fraud campaigns.

This international law enforcement operation, known as Operation Lightning, seized servers and froze cryptocurrency assets while disconnecting infected devices from the network. The takedown significantly degrades the infrastructure available to cybercriminals for obfuscating their online operations.

breaking_news.title: ClickFix techniques evolve in new infostealer campaigns
breaking_news.summary:
Cybercriminals are evolving ClickFix social engineering techniques to deliver infostealer malware, weaponizing over 250 compromised WordPress sites across 12 countries. The campaign uses fake Cloudflare CAPTCHA challenges to trick visitors into executing malicious commands, including a notable compromise of a US Senate candidate's webpage.

This large-scale execution suggests a high level of automation by the threat actor, targeting regional news outlets and local businesses to maximize victim reach. The use of stealthy in-memory payloads and alternative execution methods like Windows Terminal indicates an increasing sophistication in evasion tactics.

breaking_news.title: Interpol Operation Synergia III leads to 94 arrests and 45,000 IP takedowns
breaking_news.summary:
INTERPOL announced the results of Operation Synergia III, a global cybercrime crackdown involving 72 countries that dismantled 45,000 malicious IP addresses and servers. The operation led to 94 arrests and the seizure of 212 electronic devices linked to phishing, malware, and ransomware networks.

This coordinated effort demonstrates the effectiveness of international collaboration in disrupting criminal infrastructure and holding suspects accountable. The dismantling of such a vast network reduces the immediate threat landscape for organizations and individuals targeted by these cybercriminal groups.
